contribution.title: Mass of Sgr A* via stellar orbits
contribution.description: Tracked stars orbiting the supermassive black hole at the center of the Milky Way, measuring its mass via Kepler's third law.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Anchors the observational_constraint tier for Sgr A* (~4.1 million solar masses).

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Long-baseline observational campaigns; track real objects over decades and let the data settle the theory.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: What is the simplest dynamical model that fits this orbit?
What systematics could fake this signal?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Long-baseline programs are vulnerable to instrumentation changes.

reference.citation: Ghez, A. M. et al. (2008). Measuring distance and properties of the Milky Way's central supermassive black hole. Astrophys. J. 689, 1044.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Using Sgr A* as an anchor for supermassive black hole parameters.
how_to_apply.cautions: Sgr A* parameters carry real uncertainties; quote them with error bars.
